---
title: "Evaluation Report"
---

This report assesses the visualization prototype against the success criteria defined in
the [Project Charta](project_charta.qmd). It documents what works, what is limited, and
what remains for post-submission refinement.

---

## Success Criteria Assessment

The project charta defines four success criteria. The table below documents the current
status of each.

| Criterion | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Research question answered visually and analytically | ✅ Partial | Betweenness centrality identifies structural bridge actors in the sampled corpus. The force-directed graph makes cluster structure and bridge positions visually interpretable. Results are preliminary pending entity resolution. |
| Dataset meets course requirements (100+ obs., 6+ features, mixed types) | ✅ Met | Sample contains several thousand documents across multiple strata. The node/edge dataset has 6+ features (id, label, type, degree, occurrences, betweenness, community, dataset_ids) with both numerical (betweenness, weight) and categorical (type, community) variables. |
| Full pipeline is reproducible via GitHub and documented in Quarto | ✅ Met | All pipeline scripts are in `eda/`, runnable standalone via `uv run python eda/0X_*.py`. Dependencies and environment captured in `pyproject.toml` + `uv.lock`. This documentation auto-deploys via GitHub Actions. |
| Visualization product is publicly deployed and accessible | ✅ Met | React + Cosmograph frontend deployed at data.kulakci.de via GitHub Pages. |

---

## What Works

- **Pipeline reproducibility:** the four-step pipeline (sample → EDA → NER → network) is
  fully scripted, documented, and runnable from a clean environment.
- **Structural signal:** betweenness centrality reliably surfaces a small number of
  high-degree hub actors. The most central entities in the sampled corpus align with
  known key figures in the Epstein case, providing a basic sanity check.
- **Community structure:** Louvain community detection identifies meaningful clusters
  (legal institutions, financial entities, personal associates). The clustering is
  visually interpretable in the force-directed layout.
- **Provenance traceability:** every edge links back to the source document set
  (`doc_ids`), and the frontend provides direct links to DOJ source URLs.

---

## Limitations

**Entity resolution is the main methodological weakness.** The pipeline treats
"jeffrey epstein", "epstein", and "mr. epstein" as three separate nodes. In the network,
this artificially fragments the most central actor and inflates apparent betweenness of
nearby nodes. Manual alias mapping for the top 50 entities is planned but not yet
implemented.

**Sample size and coverage.** The stratified sample of 1 000 docs per stratum is a
fraction of the full 4.1 M-row corpus. Rare relationships — those documented in only a
handful of documents — may not appear in the sample at all. The pipeline is designed to
scale; rerunning with `SAMPLE_SIZE = 5000` or uncapped is the primary lever for improving
coverage.

**OCR noise.** The dataset provides pre-extracted text from scanned PDFs. Image-derived
pages can contain substantial OCR artefacts. The `en_core_web_sm` spaCy model is not
trained on OCR-noisy text, which depresses NER recall (entities missed) and precision
(garbled strings accepted as entity mentions). A larger model (`en_core_web_lg`) or
custom fine-tuning would improve results.

**Co-occurrence ≠ relationship.** Document-level co-occurrence is a coarse signal. Two
entities appearing in a long legal document may be entirely unrelated to each other in
context. Sentence-level co-occurrence or dependency-based extraction would be more
precise but significantly more expensive to compute.

---

## What Is Planned Post-Submission

- Alias mapping for the top 100 entities (entity resolution step between
  `03_extract_entities.py` and `04_build_network.py`).
- Larger sample (5 000 docs per stratum or full corpus) once compute time is acceptable.
- Temporal filtering: date extraction from metadata to enable timeline-based filtering
  in the frontend.
- Frontend polish: improved ego-network mode, shortest-path highlighting, and a narrative
  walkthrough mode alongside free exploration.
